preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: SaaS trial for AI-driven, intent-based design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and required as the first element. For a Validator at decision stage, use the minimal variant to reduce distraction and keep a persistent 'Upgrade Now' CTA — the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages — visible throughout the session.

sections.name: Hero — Conversion-Oriented Value Statement
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary (Validator × decision eligible) opens the page with a trust-anchored headline that reaffirms the product's core promise to a trial user already familiar with the product. The Validator variant includes a trust_signal (e.g., 'Trusted by 2,400+ design teams') and a with-social-proof LogoStrip beneath the CTA cluster, immediately grounding the upgrade ask in external credibility — critical for Validators who need proof before committing budget.

sections.name: Social Proof — Credibility Anchor
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar (Validator × decision eligible) reinforces the hero's promise with a featured customer quote plus recognizable logos. Validators at decision stage weigh peer validation heavily; a named quote with role and company provides the specificity they demand. Positioned immediately after the hero, it converts initial interest into confidence before deeper content follows.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Quantified Impact
sections.rationale: StatBlock (Validator × decision eligible) translates product value into hard numbers — e.g., '47% faster page builds, sourced from internal customer data Q1 2025.' For Validators at decision stage, unsourced claims erode trust, so every metric is paired with attribution. This section bridges emotional proof (testimonials) and rational proof (features), satisfying the Validator's need for measurable outcomes before they evaluate pricing.

sections.name: Feature Value Grid — Paid Plan Capabilities
sections.rationale: FeatureGrid (Validator × decision eligible) articulates what the trial user gains by upgrading, framed as outcome-oriented benefit statements rather than feature labels. Validators at decision stage need structured, scannable evidence that each capability solves a real workflow problem. Using the 3-col-icon layout keeps the argument tight and comparable — ideal for a user mentally contrasting free vs. paid.

sections.name: Testimonial — Decision-Point Trust Signal
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle (Validator × decision eligible) places a high-fidelity customer story — with photo, role, company logo — directly before pricing. For Validators at decision stage, a named peer who converted from trial to paid and achieved specific results is the most persuasive asset. Positioned at this decision moment, it lowers perceived risk of committing.

sections.name: Pricing — Upgrade Path
sections.rationale: PricingCard (Validator × decision eligible) presents clear plan options with the highlighted variant on the recommended tier. The Validator variant includes detailed feature lists and plan descriptions so the user can self-validate the investment. For a trial-to-paid conversion page at decision stage, with-comparison layout lets Validators see exactly what they unlock — removing ambiguity that stalls commitment.

sections.name: Final CTA — Conversion Capture
sections.rationale: CTABanner (Validator × decision eligible) closes the page with a full-width upgrade prompt. The Validator variant includes a reassuring subhead and trust_signal ('Cancel anytime · No long-term contract') to neutralize last-moment objections. At decision stage, this terminal CTA recaptures Validators who scrolled through the entire evidence chain and are now ready to act — or need one final nudge.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible and required as the final element. The with-cta variant is used here because this is a key conversion page; it provides a recovery CTA for Validators at decision stage who reach the bottom without converting, offering one last low-friction path to upgrade.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it. At decision funnel stage, this focuses user attention on upgrade action.

Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility at the critical page-opening moment for trial-to-paid conversion.

slots.headline: Your trial proved it. Paid unlocks the rest.
slots.subhead: MODE's intent-aware design system cuts component decision time by 52% — upgrade to keep that momentum on every project.
slots.trust_signal: Trusted by 2,400+ design teams · No credit card required to upgrade today

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This dual-signal approach maximizes credibility at the decision stage when users need reassurance before converting from trial to paid.

slots.headline: Teams that upgraded don't go back.
slots.quote: We cut our design-to-handoff cycle from 11 days to 4. MODE's intent layer means our system makes the right component call before we debate it.
slots.attribution: Priya Nair, Head of Product Design, Lattice — 2024 customer case study

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default. For a Validator deciding on upgrade, sourced metrics establish confidence in product impact.

slots.headline: What paid teams measure after 90 days.

stats.label: of paid users renew after year one

page.section: Feature Value Grid — Paid Plan Capabilities
page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default; use for 6 or 9 features. This layout articulates paid-plan capabilities clearly for decision-stage users.

slots.headline: What you unlock when you upgrade.
slots.subhead: Six capabilities your trial has been pointing you toward — now available without limits.

features.title: Intent Engine — Unlimited
features.description: Surface the right component for every context automatically, across all projects — not just the three your trial allowed.

features.title: Cross-Team Token Sync
features.description: Keep design tokens consistent across every team workspace in real time, eliminating the manual sync work that causes drift.

features.title: Usage Analytics Dashboard
features.description: See exactly which components your team uses, ignores, or overrides — so you can improve your system with evidence, not guesses.

features.title: Role-Based Access Controls
features.description: Protect your system's integrity by defining who can edit, publish, or consume components at every permission level.

features.title: Custom Pattern Library
features.description: Build and govern organization-specific patterns on top of MODE's foundation — without forking or maintaining a parallel system.

features.title: Priority Build Pipelines
features.description: Push component updates to production in under 9 minutes with dedicated build queues that don't compete with free-tier traffic.

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility — anonymous quotes carry no weight. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: I was skeptical about committing budget mid-quarter. Three weeks after upgrading, our team shipped a rebrand across 14 product surfaces in under two weeks. The trial showed us the potential — paid delivered the proof.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan clearly signals the conversion path for trial users evaluating upgrade options.

slots.description: Everything your trial unlocked — without the limits. Built for teams ready to run their design system at full scale.
features: Cross-team token sync across all workspaces
Usage analytics and component health dashboard
Custom pattern library with governance controls

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). centered layout with trust signals maximizes conversion capture at the terminal decision moment.

slots.headline: You've seen what MODE can do. Own it.
slots.subhead: Join 2,400+ design teams who upgraded from trial and measured the difference within 30 days.
slots.trust_signal: Cancel anytime · No long-term contract · 30-day money-back guarantee

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. At the decision funnel stage, this recovery mechanism prevents drop-off from trial-to-paid conversion.
